name: Soreheim Alliance

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Status is established through contribution and assignment before ease.
hospitality_rules: Guests are fed and housed according to usefulness, duty, and available communal provision.
insult_triggers: disrespect toward assigned labor or giant authority
class_expectations: everyone is expected to serve visibly; authority is measured in contribution, command, or allocation power
faith_etiquette: rites are tied to work, ambition, unity, and retribution rather than private ornamental piety
outsider_mistakes: reading communal culture as soft when enforcement is severe

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: Shifts, quotas, repair cycles, hauling, planning, and ritualized contribution define ordinary life from quarries to towers to farm zones.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: Hazard weather, extraction cycles, and communal festival periods all reassign labor and public expectation.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: Air carries slag, grain mash, machine grease, smoke, wet stone, and cooked communal meals. Sounds are horns, hammers, rolling freight, and shouted shift orders.

travel_and_transition_texture: Travel through the Alliance is dangerous, scheduled, supervised, and deeply shaped by escort expectations and work infrastructure.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Communal halls, crew bunks, family-linked barracks, heated worker houses, and contribution-marked living quarters dominate.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for whether they produce, endure, repair, and share under pressure.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Soreheim stories are forceful and communal: harsh duty, family-crew tension, dangerous work, giant oversight, and ambition tested by collective survival.
